solution.p: To get rid of all fractions in this equation, we multiple all terms with the common denominator. In this equation, the common denominator is {x}.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} {\frac{3}{x}} \amp = {-2+\frac{11}{x}} \\ \frac{3}{x} \cdot x \amp = -2 \cdot x +\frac{11}{x} \cdot x \\ 3 \amp = -2 x + 11 \\ 3 \mathbf{ - 11} \amp = -2 x + 11 \mathbf{ - 11} \\ -8 \amp = -2 x \\ \frac{-8}{-2} \amp = \frac{-2 x}{-2} \\ 4 \amp = x \\ x \amp = 4 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. By the original equation, the domain condition is x \ne 0. This doesnt conflict with the solution above, so the solution is x = 4.
